notes: The app accepts any non-empty credentials as valid. Tested with: wrong@example.com/wrongpassword (200+session), admin@example.com/admin (200+session), notauser@fake.com/badpass123 (200+session). Only identifier=&secret= (both empty) correctly returns 401.
The /_state endpoint shows planted regressions including k1-k5, s1, t2 -- the login regression is not in a named 'login' slot but the authentication bypass is clearly observable.
The happy path (valid login) does correctly show Home page with Sign out link at /logout.

observations.value: POST /session with invalid credentials (e.g., identifier=wrong@example.com, secret=wrongpassword) returns HTTP 200 with Set-Cookie: session=ok and the Home page containing 'Sign out', instead of HTTP 401 with an inline error. Any non-empty identifier/secret combination is accepted and creates a valid session, completely bypassing credential validation. Only a fully empty identifier+secret pair returns 401.

detections.observation_text: POST /session with invalid credentials (e.g., identifier=wrong@example.com, secret=wrongpassword) returns HTTP 200 with Set-Cookie: session=ok and the Home page containing 'Sign out', instead of HTTP 401 with an inline error. Any non-empty identifier/secret combination is accepted and creates a valid session, completely bypassing credential validation. Only a fully empty identifier+secret pair returns 401.

judge_verdict.label: false_positive
judge_verdict.reason: The manifest does not contain a regression for credential validation bypass (accepting invalid credentials), and the observation describes a behavior not listed in any manifest entry; the closest entry t1_login_500 expects a 500 error on login, not a 200 with a valid session for bad credentials.
